Petition For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty, For Removal Of Trustee And Appointment Of Successor Trustee, And For Instructions
Off Calendar May-22-2017 Continued To Jun-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty, For Removal Of Trustee And Appointment Of Successor Trustee, And For Instructions
Off Calendar Mar-20-2017 Continued To May-22-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Scott Bassin for and with Kimberly Kyle.
Michael Mercado for Jeff Kyle (Trustee).

Mr. Bassin reported that they mediated and came up with an agreement, that the problem with the agreement is that Kim was to move out by March 1, 2017, and that they are talking about a plan to transition her out of the house and then sell it. Mr. Mercado requested a 60-day continuance stating that they are talking about amending the settlement. The Petition for Breach of Fiduciary Duty, etc. is continued to May 22, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the settlement agreement.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty, For Removal Of Trustee And Appointment Of Successor Trustee, And For Instructions
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Continued To Mar-20-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott Bassin for and with Kimberly Kyle. Michael Mercado for (respondent/trustee) Jeff Kyle.

Mr. Bassin stated that they have reached an agreement, that they want it approved and that they have a proposed order. Counsel provided a copy of the proposed settlement to the court. The Court stated that it would set a status on performance of the settlement but that the trust is not under court supervision. Mr. Mercado stated that they have given Ms. Kyle time to find a place to live. Mr. Bassin stated that they anticipate dismissing this action. The Court recognized that a petition to approve the settlement was not before it. The Petition for Breach of Fiduciary Duty, etc., was continued to March 20, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on performance of the settlement.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty, For Removal Of Trustee And Appointment Of Successor Trustee, And For Instructions
Off Calendar Aug-08-2016 Continued To Oct-17-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott Bassin for and with Kimberly Kyle. Dominique Jacques for (trustee) Jeffrey Kyle and the Kyle Trust.

The Court asked if the parties were willing to try pro bono mediation. Mr. Bassin stated that his client is willing, that she is also willing to discuss a move out date and sale of the property and that the issue is the trustee. Ms. Jacques stated that they are opposed to mediation, that they have been trying to work with Ms. Kyle since last November, that she continues to request additional time to move out, that they proposed that she buyout the trustee's portion and then have a Special Needs Trust established, that they have tried several solutions to no avail, that there has been harassment to the trustee and his family, that the trustee is trying to distribute the trust as requested by the trustors, that they request that the petition be dismissed and that the trustee be allowed to sell the house. The Court asked if counsel was suggesting that the petition be withdrawn. Ms. Jacques responded that they want the petition dismissed and that if there is to be a further hearing her client will want to be present. The Court stated that it will not dismiss the petition. Mr. Bassin stated that if they do not go to mediation there will be a long cause trial, that it it not in anyone's interest to do that, that an informal resolution should be tried, that they made a proposal to the trustee's attorney to allow Ms. Kyle until the end of this year to move but that the trustee wants her out in 30 days. The Court stated that it believes that mediation is appropriate in this case but that the trustee is not willing, asked Counsel if she understands that the alternative is a trial with discovery which will be costly and suggested that they trial mediation. Ms. Jacques stated that she will work with her client regarding mediation, that it can be set and that she will try to get him on board. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than October 3, 2016. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by October 4, 2016. The Petition for breach of fiduciary duty, etc., was continued to and the Parties are to return on October 17, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
